---
title: "Random Forest 2026"
author: "Annabelle Dyer"
date: "2026-03-17"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```

```{r}

library(rfPermute)
library(randomForest)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)

#analysis

rp <- rfPermute(ATTRIBUTE_Phenotype ~., data = porites_standardized_phenotype, na.action = na.omit, ntree = 100, num.rep = 10)
#predicts Phenotype using all other columns (. represents all other columns)
#increase ntree and num.rep until results are accurate 
Rp
#displays confusion matrix
#Extract variable importance
importance_df <- as.data.frame(importance(rp))
importance_df$Variable <- rownames(importance_df)

#displaying results

# Extract variable importance and sort by MeanDecreaseAccuracy
importance_df <- as.data.frame(importance(rp))
importance_df$Variable <- rownames(importance_df)
importance_df <- importance_df[order(-importance_df$MeanDecreaseAccuracy), ]  # Sort in descending order

# Keep only the top 100 most important variables
top_100_importance <- importance_df[1:100, ]

# Create a color column based on B and NB values
top_100_importance$Color <- ifelse(top_100_importance$B > top_100_importance$NB, "orange", "blue")

# Create variable importance plot
ggplot(top_100_importance, aes(x = reorder(Variable, MeanDecreaseAccuracy), y = MeanDecreaseAccuracy, fill = Color)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", alpha = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_identity() +  # Uses the exact colors specified in the data
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Top 100 Variable Importance Plot",
       x = "Variables",
       y = "Mean Decrease in Accuracy") +
  theme(axis.text.y = element_blank()) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = ifelse(rank(-MeanDecreaseAccuracy) <= 10, Variable, "")),
                  hjust = 0, size = 3, nudge_y = 0.1)

```
